a:r.a:t: Impact on Cost Differential vs. Foreign
US crew cost differential vs. foreign driven by structural factors including
100% of officers and 75% of unlicensed crew must be US citizens (Market participant feedback;
Mariner shortage intensifies wage competition (Market participant feedback;
H&M premiums driven by 4-6x higher replacement cost of US-built hull vs. foreign
P&I premiums inflated by Jones Act crew injury liability

The maritime industry…is facing a new and formidable challenge: the rise of “nuclear verdicts” in personal injury cases, particularly those involving Jones Act seamen…typically defined as verdicts of $10 million or more.”
The unpredictability and magnitude of nuclear verdicts are having a direct impact on the cost and availability of insurance for maritime employers.”
R&M cost differential vs. foreign driven by policy and wage factors
Increased R&M costs due to 50% ad valorem duties on
Analogous US labor ~2-7x greater than foreign counterparts i.e., shipyard workforce (
“Purchases for or repairs made to certain vessels while they are outside the United States are subject to declaration, entry, and payment of ad valorem duty.”

p:cNvPr.name: Picture 268
p:cNvPr.descr:
The image depicts a text display showing an average annual salary of $222,921 with a potential bonus of up to $71,090.
AI-generated content may be incorrect.

p:cNvPr.name: Picture 270
p:cNvPr.descr:
The image displays a figure, presumably a ship's officer, with text indicating an average annual salary of $156,502 and a bonus potential of up to $57,414.
AI-generated content may be incorrect.

p:cNvPr.name: Picture 272
p:cNvPr.descr:
Average Annual Salary: $89,947
AI-generated content may be incorrect.

p:cNvPr.name: Picture 278
p:cNvPr.descr:
The image depicts a visual representation of an electronics technician's job, highlighting an average annual salary of $148,763 with an additional bonus of $67,243.
AI-generated content may be incorrect.

a:r.a:t: Military Sealift Command job postings substantiate US rates, and commercial operators pay ~10-20% above MSC (
JM: Bold row titles as appropriate
JM: Differing font sizes acceptable to emphasize certain points and/or if certain cells have minimal info
JM: Bullet lists; use dots for 1
JM: Use dashes for second level
JM: All images should have shadow in lower right hand side; ensure images are the same size
JM: No note / source box acceptable if sources are included in content (useful for tables)
JM: Place verbatims / quotes in quotation marks and italicize
JM: Denote gaps in quotes with ellipses
Top border - 1 pt, solid, true black
Internal borders – ½ pt, solid (for content), gray #5 from template color (LHS color scale, at the bottom - #7F7F7F)
Generally avoid vertical borders for content, but may be acceptable in certain circumstances - reference other materials for examples
